items.snippet: Neo is Bhavin Turakhia’s fifth venture and his latest involving enterprise software. This time he's taking on Microsoft Office and Google Apps with AI.

items.title: SpaceX has an AI device prototype, and it sure sounds phone-ish
items.snippet: SpaceX reportedly showed investors a "handset-like" AI device before going public. It could be another signal SpaceX wants to expand into wireless.

items.title: Ashton Kutcher leaving Sound Ventures to launch new VC firm with Morgan Beller
items.snippet: Sound built its reputation on concentrated, high-conviction bets in category-leading AI labs, while Kutcher's new fund appears to be chasing the layer underneath those companies — the infrastructure and energy that power them.

items.title: Cloudflare’s new policy pushes AI companies to pay for publishers’ content
items.snippet: Cloudflare is giving AI companies until September 15 to separate web crawlers used for search from those used for AI training and agents, or risk being blocked by default on many publisher sites.

items.snippet: Venice AI is already profitable, with annualized run-rate revenues of over $70 million, CEO Erik Voorhees said.

items.title: Meta, like SpaceX, looks to turn excess AI compute into cash
items.snippet: Meta is developing plans for a cloud infrastructure business, selling access to AI compute power and models. The move would pit it against the big cloud providers like Amazon Web Services, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure.
